title: Persist Data With a Volume
description:
## Mount a Named Volume

Named volumes let container data outlive the container.

**Requirements:**
1. Create a named volume called **`data-vol`**
2. Run an **`alpine`** container named **`vbox`** in the background (e.g. `sleep 3600` to keep it alive)
3. Mount the **`data-vol`** volume at **`/data`** inside the container

> 💡 Create the volume with `docker volume create`, then mount it with `-v data-vol:/data` on `docker run`.

hints.title: Create the volume
hints.body: A named volume is created independently of any container.

hints.title: Run a container with the volume mounted
hints.body: Keep a lightweight container alive with sleep and mount the volume at /data.
hints.command: docker run -d --name vbox -v data-vol:/data alpine sleep 3600

validation.description: Checks that the 'data-vol' volume exists and is mounted into the 'vbox' container.
